diagrams.title: Unix System Architecture Layers
diagrams.description: Shows the hierarchical relationship from hardware through kernel to user applications, highlighting where system calls fit in the architecture.
elements: Kernel (System calls interface, Process Management, Memory Management, File System, Device Drivers)
C Library (libc - wrappers like fopen, printf)
Arrow showing User Mode → Kernel Mode transition at system call boundary
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the separation between user space and kernel space, understanding that system calls bridge these two domains.

diagrams.title: System Call Execution Flow
diagrams.description: Step-by-step diagram showing what happens when a program calls a system call like read().
elements: 3. System call instruction (syscall) triggers mode switch
7. Return to user mode with result
diagrams.purpose: Understand the complete path from user request to kernel action, including the critical mode transition that defines system programming.
